messages.content:
The purpose of this exercise is to practice writing without cliches.

Rewrite the following sentences:

1) The European market is extremely volatile at the moment and we need to avoid it like the plague.

2) Our new product is selling like hotcakes!

3) Entering the China market is a no brainer.

4) We need to start using social media for our marketing in an way, shape or form. 

5) Our deadline for product launch is fast approaching which means our team has to burn the midnight oil.

1) The European market is currently exhibiting considerable fluctuation; we must steer clear of it.

2) Our new product is selling quickly and in high demand!

3) Expanding into the China market is a straightforward decision.

4) We must incorporate social media into our marketing strategy in some capacity.

5) The deadline for our product launch looms overhead, necessitating our team to work late into the night.

Question 1
The phrase ‘attractive, blonde bus-driver’ could easily be considered gender insensitive because______.

a. Women are employed as bus drivers as often as men.


b. The gender and appearance of the bus driver is irrelevant in terms of his/her occupation and performance.


c. Men are better bus drivers than women.
b. The gender and appearance of the bus driver is irrelevant in terms of his/her occupation and performance.

The usual tone and style used for a memo should be:

Serious


Formal, semi-formal, neutral


Informal
Formal, semi-formal, neutral
What is the noun form of 'transmit'?
The noun form of "transmit" is "transmission."

Which sentence is using the active voice?

Quarterly results will be published in the next financial report.


The finance department will publish the quarterly results in the next financial report.
The second sentence, "The finance department will publish the quarterly results in the next financial report" is using the active voice.

You should always use the active voice in your writing.


True


False

False. While the active voice is generally preferred in most types of writing, there are certain situations in which the passive voice may be more appropriate or effective. It is important to use both the active and passive voice appropriately based on the specific needs of the text.

Which sentence(s) is grammatically correct?


The economy seems to be improving; therefore, we should anticipate higher growth this year.


The economy seems to be improving. Therefore, we should anticipate higher growth this year.


The economy seems to be improving, therefore, we should anticipate higher growth this year.
Both the first and second sentences are grammatically correct, but the third sentence is not. The third sentence can be corrected by using a semicolon or by splitting it into two separate sentences.

Which sentence(s) is grammatically correct?

1 point

The farm lands have not been productive since they were destroyed in the war.


Since they were destroyed in the war, the farm lands have not been productive.


The farm lands have not been productive. Since these were destroyed in the war.


Since the farm lands were destroyed in the war, they have not been productive.
The first and second sentences are both grammatically correct. The third sentence does not sound natural. The fourth sentence is grammatically correct, but it changes the emphasis of the sentence.

Identify the five grammar errors and three vocabulary errors made in the memo below. Rewrite the memo and submit your revision. Provide a brief explanation of what you changed and why.

Senior management has noticed frequent absences and tardiness of employees for the past two months. Which is detrimental to the efficient framework of this company, please remind employees concerned to promote strictly the regulations on attendance.

In our previous discussion dated (January 05, 2014), we reviewed your attendance records. Please take note that you have been issued a memo for poor attendance record. In the future, please be clear of the following: five (5) consecutive days of delay will result in a 3-day suspension and three (3) absences without valid reason will be result in terminated.

To avoid any misunderstanding on your part about how serious this matter is. Consider this your final warning. If you will to miss work in the future, please to notify the management directly, you will not be allowed to report back to work without presenting a valid medical certificate. Any further violation of this policy will result in termination.

Regards,

Senior management 

ABC Incorporated

Incorrect Memo:
Senior management has noticed frequent absences and tardiness of employees for the past two months. Which is detrimental to the efficient framework of this company, please remind employees concerned to promote strictly the regulations on attendance.

In our previous discussion dated (January 05, 2014), we reviewed your attendance records. Please take note that you have been issued a memo for poor attendance record. In the future, please be clear of the following: five (5) consecutive days of delay will result in a 3-day suspension and three (3) absences without valid reason will be result in terminated.

To avoid any misunderstanding on your part about how serious this matter is. Consider this your final warning. If you will to miss work in the future, please to notify the management directly, you will not be allowed to report back to work without presenting a valid medical certificate. Any further violation of this policy will result in termination.

Regards,

Senior management

ABC Incorporated

Corrected Memo:
To: All employees
From: Senior Management
Subject: Attendance Policy

Senior management has noticed an increase in employee absences and tardiness for the past two months, which is detrimental to the efficient framework of this company. Employees are reminded to strictly adhere to the regulations on attendance to ensure the continued smooth operation of our business.

In our previous meeting on January 5th, 2014, we reviewed your attendance records. Please note that you were issued a memo for poor attendance. Please be aware that five (5) consecutive days of tardiness will result in a three-day suspension, and three (3) absences without valid reason will result in termination.

To avoid any confusion regarding the severity of this matter, please consider this your final warning. If you will miss work in the future, please notify management directly. You will not be allowed to report back to work without presenting a valid medical certificate. Any further violation of this policy will result in termination.

Sincerely,

Senior Management
ABC Incorporated

Explanation: 

Grammar Errors:
1. The incorrect use of "Which" instead of "This" in the first sentence.
2. The incorrect use of the word "remind" in the first sentence instead of "to remind".
3. The lack of clarity and organization in the message. The third paragraph is confusing, as the warning about a 3-day suspension is not logically linked to the previous sentence.
4. The use of "will to" instead of "fail to" in the fourth paragraph.
5. The use of "terminated" instead of "termination".

Vocabulary Errors:
1. The use of "Concerned" instead of "with attendance".
2. The use of "to avoid any misunderstanding on your part" instead of "to ensure clear understanding".
3. The phrase "please to notify the management directly" is incorrect. It should be "please notify management directly".

In the revised memo, I corrected the grammar and vocabulary errors found in the original memo. I also reorganized the text to make the message clear and concise.

Write an internal memo to all the staff in Emerald Lake Resort informing them of a change in the 'No Smoking' policy. You are a Human Resources manager for Emerald Lake Resort. This memo should be between 10-18 sentences long, not including any headings you choose to insert to aid in organization.

[INTERNAL MEMO]

To: All Emerald Lake Resort Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Update on the 'No Smoking' Policy

Dear All,

I am writing to inform you of an important update to our 'No Smoking' policy. As you know, Emerald Lake Resort has a strict policy against smoking in all public areas of the resort. However, we have received feedback from guests regarding secondhand smoke in certain outdoor areas of the resort.

In response to this feedback, we have decided to expand our 'No Smoking' policy to include all outdoor areas of the resort. Starting on the 1st of next month, the new policy will take effect and all outdoor areas will be designated as "No Smoking" zones.

We want to emphasize that this decision was made with the safety and comfort of our guests and staff in mind. We hope that this updated policy will help maintain a clean, healthy and respectful environment for all guest and staff members.

We also want to remind everyone that smoking is a personal choice and we respect everyone's right to make that choice. At the same time, we want to remind staff members that they have a responsibility to maintain the policies and procedures of Emerald Lake Resort, including the new 'No Smoking' policy.

In addition to the update in policy, Emerald Lake Resort will be offering support to those staff members who wish to quit smoking. We understand that quitting can be a difficult process and we are here to support our staff on this journey.

If you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact the Human Resources department. We value your cooperation in this matter and appreciate your commitment to upholding policies that enhance the experience of our guests.

Sincerely,

Human Resource Manager
Emerald Lake Resort

The graph below shows the annual carbon emissions according to region between 1980 to 2005.

 Please write a summary of the data and information by highlighting and explaining important factors or relationships. Write about comparisons that are noteworthy and include a brief summary and conclusion. 

https://www.flickr.com/photos/mplemmon/3203403862

The graph shows the annual carbon emissions by region between 1980 and 2005. Overall, the total carbon emissions from all regions has increased drastically over the years, with some regions experiencing a steeper increase than others.

The most significant finding is that the Asia Pacific region has seen an enormous increase in carbon emissions over the years and has surpassed the Western Europe region as the largest emitter by 2005. In 1980, the Asia Pacific's carbon emissions were lower than that of Western Europe and North America; however, by 2005, they were much higher, which can be attributed to their rapid economic development and population growth.

Another region that has seen a significant increase in carbon emissions is the Middle East and Africa, which has likely been driven by the increased use of fossil fuels for energy and transportation. On the other hand, carbon emissions from North America have remained relatively constant over the years, while Europe has seen a slight decrease in emissions since the early 1990s.

Overall, the data highlights the need for a global effort to reduce carbon emissions, with a particular focus on the rapidly developing economies of the Asia Pacific and the Middle East and Africa regions. While some regions may have reduced their carbon emissions, the steady increase in the total emissions from all regions is alarming considering the negative impact it has on the environment. Therefore, it is of utmost importance that each country, as well as international organizations, take actions to decrease carbon emissions to mitigate climate change.

Choose the BEST word to complete the following sentence: The company hopes that such measures will ____ its position in the market.

premise


saturate


merge


consolidate
consolidate

Choose the BEST word to complete the following sentence: Both companies have agreed to ____ and will present their cases to the court.

remuneration


obligation


preparation


arbitration
arbitration

The following words all have the basic meaning of ‘small’. Discuss which ones you think are neutral – that is, have no added meaning? Which ones have positive or negative connotations? Of course, these connotations may vary from country to country!

small, dinky, minute, tiny, puny

petite, pint-size, little, dwarfish, bijou

Neutral meaning: small, little, minute, tiny.

Positive connotations: petite, bijou.

Negative connotations: puny, dwarfish. 

The other words - dinky and pint-size - may be neutral or may have either positive or negative connotations, depending on the context.

At its heart, an executive summary is a _____________ document.

persuasive 


argumentative


theoretical
Persuasive

C.
1) a brief background statement on the company which is related to the problem the company is experiencing;
2) an articulation of the specific problem the company is experiencing; and
3) a statement of the goals the company has related to solving the problem.

In the executive summary for a business proposal, it is important to provide an overview of the problems your potential client is experiencing because:

you want to focus the client's attention and convince them that these problems need to be addressed, preferably using the solutions you are proposing.


you want to explain why you are the best person to solve these problems.


you want to show you have done research on the potential client before you wrote the proposal.
you want to focus the client's attention and convince them that these problems need to be addressed, preferably using the solutions you are proposing.

When writing the content of an executive summary one should focus on providing _________

a time-line of the work you propose to provide. 


a detailed explanation of the
details of your proposal with specific technical information.


a motivational speech to encourage your potential client to buy your services.


an overview of your business proposal, focusing
on organizational issues and outcomes.
an overview of your business proposal, focusing on organizational issues and outcomes.

In the executive summary for a business proposal, you can attract the potential client’s
attention by ________

writing a clear description of the company’s background and history.


solving the problem or meeting the need the potential client is experiencing. 


showing you understand the specific problem or need that is affecting the company’s profitability or productivity.
showing you understand the specific problem or need that is affecting the company’s profitability or productivity.
